title: Former Bruins coach Bruce Cassidy abruptly fired by Vegas before playoffs
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruce Cassidy was fired by the Vegas Golden Knights less than a month before the Stanley Cup Playoffs.
John Tortorella replaces Cassidy as the coach of the Golden Knights, amid a rough season-ending stretch.
Cassidy helped Vegas win its first Stanley Cup in the 2022-23 season after being released by the Bruins.

title: New NFL mock draft has Patriots addressing major need on defense with 31st pick
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Patriots may select Clemson defensive end T.J. Parker with the 31st pick in the 2026 NFL Draft.
Parker recorded 21.5 sacks, 126 tackles, and six forced fumbles in three seasons at Clemson.
The Patriots aim to boost their pass rush, having recorded only 35 sacks last year, ranking low in the league.

title: North Shore police department asking for public’s help after theft of historical plaques
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Newburyport police investigate theft of a historical plaque from the Old Hill graveyard's African American memorial.
Thefts include missing plaques from Oak Hill Cemetery, Cashman Park, and Atkinson Common since January.
Police seek public assistance and provide contact number for potential tips on thefts.

title: Bruins sign Merrimack goalie Max Lundgren to entry-level contract
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins signed Merrimack College goalie Max Lundgren to a one-year entry-level contract with a $952,500 NHL cap hit.
Lundgren, 23, hails from Angelholm, Sweden, and was the Hockey East tournament MVP, posting a .920 save percentage this past season.
Lundgren led Merrimack to its first Hockey East title, setting program records for saves and minutes played in a season.

title: 2 people from Mass. killed when helicopter crashes in Hawaii
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Margaret Rimmler, 65, and Patrick Haskell, 59, from Massachusetts, died in a helicopter crash in Hawaii.
The crash involved five people, occurring off Kalalau Beach, Kauai, where three were killed and two survived.
The helicopter, a Hughes OH-6 Cayuse, was operated by Airborne Aviation, a local tour company in Kauai.

title: Review & setlist: Diana Krall brought a special kind of magic to The Cabot
author: Marc Hirsh
bullets: Diana Krall performed at The Cabot in Beverly with bassist John Clayton on March 28, 2026.
Krall's setlist included standards like "Almost Like Being In Love" and "I've Got You Under My Skin."
The concert featured Krall's piano and vocal talents, with a performance style described as both intimate and expansive.

title: Chelsea teen charged with murder after allegedly chasing down, stabbing man
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Sergio Josue Castellanos Cerrato, 18, charged with murder after allegedly chasing and stabbing Eduardo Alfredo Rosales Lopez, 25, in Chelsea.
The incident occurred on March 19 near Chestnut Street and Everett Avenue, with Lopez dying from injuries in the hospital.
Castellanos Cerrato was arraigned and held without bail, with the next court date set for April 21.

title: 2025 marked Boston area’s slowest year of post-pandemic population growth, data shows
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston's population growth in 2025 was the slowest since 2021, increasing by only 11,991 people.
Massachusetts saw its smallest population increase since 2021, with a growth of 15,524 from 2024 to 2025.
Middlesex and Worcester counties had the highest growth but still saw significant declines from previous years.

title: North Atlantic right whales now have a GoFundMe
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: The Marineland Right Whale Project launched a GoFundMe to raise $15,000 for North Atlantic right whale conservation.
The fundraiser supports research by Associated Scientists at Woods Hole, targeting vessel strikes and fishing gear entanglement.
North Atlantic right whales are critically endangered, with only a few hundred remaining, facing threats from human activities.

title: 5 times the Patriots traded for wide receivers in recent history, and how each acquisition panned out
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Randy Moss had a record-breaking first season with the Patriots, acquired from the Raiders for a fourth-round pick.
Wes Welker became a prolific slot receiver for New England after being traded from the Miami Dolphins in 2007.
Josh Gordon showed potential with over 700 yards in 11 games in 2018 but faced suspension issues limiting his impact.
